title: Worker that uses {:cancel, reason} when the target no longer exists
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.WebhookRetryWorker` (queue `:webhooks`, max_attempts 10). perform/1 takes `%{"webhook_id" => id}`. Call `MyApp.Webhooks.get(id)`. If the webhook is not found, return `{:cancel, :webhook_deleted}` — there's no point retrying a deleted target. If found, call `MyApp.Webhooks.deliver(webhook)`. On `{:error, :rate_limited, retry_after}`, return `{:snooze, retry_after}`. On any other `{:error, reason}`, return `{:error, reason}`. On `:ok`, return `:ok`. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/webhook_retry_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Full return-value ladder with the `:cancel` on deletion — tests that the author recognizes 'target gone' as a cancel condition, not an error.
